title: प्रत्यंगिरा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ अथ श्रीप्रत्यंगिरास्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ क्ष्रौं ह्रीं प्रत्यंगिरायै नमः।

नमस्ते प्रत्यंगिरे देवि सिंहवक्त्रे महोदरे।
सहस्रशिरसे तुभ्यं नमोऽत्यन्तभयापहे॥१॥

प्रत्यंगिरां महाघोरां सिंहमुखीं दिगम्बरीम्।
ज्वालामालाकुलां देवीं प्रणमामि पुनः पुनः॥२॥

शत्रूणां शत्रुरूपां त्वां कृत्याकर्षणकारिणीम्।
अभिचारविनाशार्थं प्रत्यंगिरां नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

मन्त्रतन्त्रप्रहारघ्नीं यन्त्रदोषनिवारिणीम्।
दृष्टिदोषहरां देवीं प्रत्यंगिरां भजाम्यहम्॥४॥

सर्वग्रहनिवारिण्यै सर्वव्याधिहरां शुभाम्।
सर्वशत्रुविनाशार्थं प्रत्यंगिरां नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

नरसिंहस्य या शक्तिर्महाप्रत्यंगिरा परा।
सर्वदुष्टविनाशार्थं तामहं शरणं व्रजे॥६॥

अथर्ववेदसंभूतां महाविद्यां सुदुर्लभाम्।
कृत्याभिचारदाहार्थं प्रत्यंगिरां नमाम्यहम्॥७॥

नृसिंहरूपधारिण्यै उग्रसिंहमुखीं पराम्।
सहस्रबाहुयुक्तां च प्रत्यंगिरां भजाम्यहम्॥८॥

यो वा मां शापयेत्कश्चिन्मन्त्रतन्त्रबलेन वा।
सा देवी तं विनाशार्थं प्रतिशापं प्रयच्छति॥९॥

ॐ प्रत्यंगिरे प्रत्यंगिरे
परमन्त्र परतन्त्र परयन्त्र परशापनिवारिणि।
परविद्वेषनिकृन्तनि
ॐ क्ष्रौं ह्रीं हुं फट् स्वाहा॥१०॥

प्रत्यंगिरास्तवं पुण्यं यः पठेद्भक्तिमान्नरः।
सर्वाभिचारनाशं च सर्वारिष्टविनाशनम्।
कवचं तस्य देवेशी सर्वदा सन्निधा भवेत्॥

॥ इति श्रीप्रत्यंगिरास्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Shri Pratyangiraa Stotram ||

Om Kshraum Hreem Pratyangiraayai Namah.

Namaste Pratyangire Devi Simhavaktre Mahodare.
Sahasrashirase Tubhyam Namotyantabhayaapahe.||1||

Pratyangiraam Mahaghoraam Simhamukheem Digambareem.
Jvaalaamaalaakulaam Deveem Pranamami Punah Punah.||2||

Shatrunaam Shatruoopaam Tvaam Krityaakarshana-Kaarineem.
Abhichaaravinashaartham Pratyangiraam Namaamyaham.||3||

Mantratantrapraharaghneem Yantradoshanivaarineem.
Drishtidoshaharaam Deveem Pratyangiraam Bhajaamyaham.||4||

Sarvagrahanivaarinyai Sarvavyaadhiharaam Shubhaam.
Sarvashatruvinashaartham Pratyangiraam Namaamyaham.||5||

Narasimhasya Yaa Shaktir-Mahaa-Pratyangiraa Paraa.
Sarvadushtavinashaartham Taamaham Sharanam Vraje.||6||

AtharvavedaSambhootaam Mahaavidyaam Sudurlabhaam.
Krityaabhichaaradaahaartham Pratyangiraam Namaamyaham.||7||

Nrisimharoopdhaarineem Ugrasimhaamukeem Paraam.
Sahasrabaahuyuktaam Cha Pratyangiraam Bhajaamyaham.||8||

Yo Vaa Maam Shaapayetkaschhin-Mantratantrabalena Vaa.
Saa Devee Tam Vinashaartham Pratishaapam Prayachchhati.||9||

Om Pratyangire Pratyangire
Paramantra Paratantra Parayantra Parashaapanivaarini.
Paravidveshaniikritani
Om Kshraum Hreem Hum Phat Svaahaa.||10||

Pratyangiraa-Stavam Punyam Yah Pathedbhaktimaan Narah.
Sarvabhichaaranasham Cha Sarvaarishta-Vinaashanam.
Kavacham Tasya Deveshee Sarvadaa Sannidhaa Bhavet.

|| Iti Shri Pratyangiraa Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
मूल मन्त्र: ॐ क्ष्रौं ह्रीं प्रत्यंगिरायै नमः।

१. हे प्रत्यंगिरा देवी! सिंहवक्त्रे, महोदरे, सहस्रशिरसे — अत्यन्तभयापहारिणी को नमस्कार।

२. महाघोर, सिंहमुखी, दिगम्बरी, ज्वालामाला से आकुल देवी प्रत्यंगिरा को बार-बार प्रणाम।

३. शत्रुओं के शत्रु-रूपा, कृत्या (काला जादू) आकर्षण करने वाली — अभिचार विनाश के लिए प्रत्यंगिरा को नमस्कार।

४. मन्त्र-तन्त्र प्रहार को नष्ट करने वाली, यन्त्र-दोष निवारिणी, दृष्टि-दोष (नज़र) हरने वाली — प्रत्यंगिरा को भजता हूँ।

५. सर्वग्रह निवारिणी, सर्वव्याधि हरने वाली शुभा — सर्वशत्रु विनाश के लिए प्रत्यंगिरा को नमस्कार।

६. नृसिंह की शक्ति जो महा-प्रत्यंगिरा परा हैं — सर्वदुष्ट विनाश के लिए उनकी शरण लेता हूँ।

७. अथर्ववेद से सम्भूत, अत्यन्त दुर्लभ महाविद्या — कृत्या-अभिचार दहन के लिए प्रत्यंगिरा को नमस्कार।

८. नृसिंहरूपधारिणी, उग्रसिंहमुखी, परा, सहस्रबाहुयुक्ता — प्रत्यंगिरा को भजता हूँ।

९. जो कोई मुझे मन्त्र-तन्त्र बल से शाप दे — वह देवी उसके विनाशार्थ प्रतिशाप देती हैं।

१०. ॐ प्रत्यंगिरे! पर-मन्त्र, पर-तन्त्र, पर-यन्त्र, पर-शाप निवारिणी, पर-विद्वेष निकृन्तनी — ॐ क्ष्रौं ह्रीं हुं फट् स्वाहा।

जो भक्तिमान मनुष्य इस पुण्य प्रत्यंगिरा-स्तव का पाठ करता है, उसके सर्वाभिचार और सर्वारिष्ट नष्ट होते हैं। देवेशी कवच-रूपिणी उसके सन्निकट सदा रहती हैं।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीpratyangirastotramमन्त्रस्य अथर्ववेदोक्त ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् एवं मन्त्र छन्दः। श्री प्रत्यंगिरा देवी देवता। ॐ क्ष्रौं ह्रीं बीजम्। प्रत्यंगिरा शक्ति (प्रतिशापकारिणी) शक्तिः। सर्वाभिचार-नाश एवं सर्वारिष्ट-निवारण कीलकम्। श्रीप्रत्यंगिरा देवीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Pratyangira is the lion-faced Goddess — Narasimha's Shakti — supreme counter-force
Most powerful prayer for reversing black magic, curses, and abhichara (sorcery)
Traditionally recited for removal of evil eye (drishti dosha) and malefic planetary effects
Returns curses and negative energies back to the sender
Born from the Atharva Veda — extremely rare and potent
Traditionally invoked to establish an impenetrable spiritual shield around the devotee
Nullifies negative mantras, tantras, and yantras directed at the devotee
description: Pratyangira Stotram — प्रत्यंगिरा स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Durga. Attributed to Atharva Veda tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to return curses and negative energies back to the sender and born from the Atharva Veda — extremely rare and potent. It is also said to pratyangira is the lion-faced Goddess — Narasimha's Shakti — supreme counter-force. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Navratri and Narasimha Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. With 10 verses and a reading time of about 6 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Pratyangira Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Pratyangira Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer to the lion-faced Goddess for reversing black magic and total protection.
relatedSearches: Pratyangira Stotram Sanskrit lyrics PDF download
Maha Pratyangira mantra Om Kshraum Hreem
Pratyangira homa for black magic removal
Pratyangira for evil eye drishti dosha protection

seoFaqs.question: What is the Pratyangira Stotram and who is Goddess Pratyangira?
seoFaqs.answer: The Pratyangira Stotram is a ten-verse Sanskrit hymn opening with the bija-mantra Om Kshraum Hreem Pratyangirayai Namah, dedicated to Goddess Pratyangira — the lion-faced (simha-mukhi), thousand-headed (sahasra-shirsha), thousand-armed (sahasra-bahu), sky-clad (digambari) form of the Devi who is regarded as the Shakti of Lord Narasimha. She is invoked specifically for the reversal of black magic, curses, abhichara (sorcery), evil eye, and hostile mantra-tantra-yantra. The hymn declares her to have emerged from the Atharva Veda (Atharva-veda-sambhutam mahavidyam) as an exceedingly rare and potent vidya. (Sources: Atharva Veda tradition; Tantric Devi anthologies; Hindupedia Pratyangira.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Pratyangira called Narasimha's Shakti and what is the Sharabha story?
seoFaqs.answer: Pratyangira is intimately connected to Lord Narasimha through the famous Sharabha-Narasimha-Pratyangira myth narrated in several Puranas. After Narasimha slew Hiranyakashipu, his fury did not subside and threatened the cosmos. Lord Shiva manifested as Sharabha — a fierce eight-legged part-lion, part-bird, part-human form — to pacify Narasimha. From Sharabha then emerged Goddess Pratyangira (also called Atharvana Bhadrakali or Narasimhi), who took the wrath of Narasimha into herself and absorbed his fury, restoring cosmic balance. She is therefore the absorber and reverser of all destructive cosmic forces, including hostile sorcery aimed at devotees. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Sharabha Upanishad; Atharvana Rahasya.)

seoFaqs.question: Is initiation required to recite the Pratyangira Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: An important traditional caveat applies. The full Pratyangira sadhana — including the principal Maha Pratyangira mantra (much longer than the bija given in this stotra), the elaborate yantra worship, the prati-shapa (counter-curse) homa procedure, and the Pratyangira Mala Mantra — is a strict tantric upasana that requires diksha (initiation) from a qualified guru in the Atharvana, Shri Vidya, or Shakta lineages. The stotra itself, being a devotional praise-hymn, is generally accessible for sincere devotees to recite as bhakti and protective prayer. Devotees seeking the deeper sadhana should approach an authentic acharya, since incorrect Pratyangira sadhana is traditionally said to backfire on the practitioner. (Sources: traditional Atharvana tantra guidance; Sholinghur temple acharyas.)

seoFaqs.question: Where are the famous Pratyangira temples located?
seoFaqs.answer: The most famous Pratyangira temple in India is the Sri Sholinghur Yoga Narasimha-Pratyangira temple in Tamil Nadu, where Pratyangira is worshipped on a hill (Periya Malai) alongside the main Narasimha shrine — uniquely combining both deities in their inseparable Narasimha-Shakti relationship. Other important shrines include the Ashta Bhuja Pratyangira temple at Mettur in Tamil Nadu, the Pratyangira Devi temple at Kalpathy in Palakkad Kerala, the Maha Pratyangira temple at Ayyavadi near Kumbhakonam, and Pratyangira shrines within Mahavidya complexes in Bengal and Assam. These temples are pilgrimage centres for those seeking protection from black magic and chronic adversity. (Sources: Tamil Nadu temple guides; Sholinghur temple history.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Atharva Veda connection of Pratyangira?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 7 declares Atharva-veda-sambhutam mahavidyam su-durlabham — born from the Atharva Veda, an extremely rare great vidya. The Atharva Veda is traditionally the repository of protective, healing and counter-magical mantras (rakshobhna and abhichara-pratikriya) — distinct from the more sacrificial-ritual focus of the Rig, Yajur and Sama Vedas. Pratyangira's identity as the supreme prati-abhichara (counter-sorcery) shakti makes her the natural Devi of the Atharva tradition. The Atharvana Rahasya and Atharvana Pratyangira Sukta texts preserve her core mantras and procedures, and her sadhana is considered the apex of Atharvana tantric practice. (Sources: Atharva Veda; Atharvana Rahasya; Pancharatra Atharvana traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Pratyangira Stotram phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti declares that the devoted reciter obtains sarvabhichara-nasham (destruction of all sorcery directed at them), sarva-arishta-vinashanam (destruction of all calamities and inauspicious omens), and the personal kavacha (armor) of the Devi who remains constantly near (sarvada sannidha bhavet). Verses 4-5 specifically mention the removal of yantra-dosha (defects in others' yantras directed at the devotee), drishti-dosha (evil eye), sarva-graha (all planetary afflictions), sarva-vyadhi (all diseases), and sarva-shatru (all enemies). Verse 9 promises the active prati-shapa — the curse of any hostile sorcerer being returned to its source. (Sources: text phalashruti; Atharvana Pratyangira commentaries.)
